evals.prompt:
I have a DSPy program that classifies support tickets. It was working fine last week but now I get 'ValueError: Could not parse output' on about half the inputs. I did not change any code. Using openai/gpt-4o-mini. Here is the signature:

class ClassifyTicket(dspy.Signature):
    ticket_text: str = dspy.InputField()
    category: Literal['billing', 'technical', 'account'] = dspy.OutputField()
    urgency: Literal['low', 'medium', 'high'] = dspy.OutputField()

I optimized it with BootstrapFewShot and load from classify_ticket.json.
evals.expected_output: Debugging guidance that starts with inspecting dspy.inspect_history() to see what the LM is actually returning. Should consider that loaded demos from classify_ticket.json might be stale or that a model update changed output formatting. Should NOT immediately rewrite the signature or switch models.

assertions.name: uses_inspect_history
assertions.description: Recommends dspy.inspect_history() to see the raw LM output before making changes

assertions.name: checks_saved_state
assertions.description: Considers that loaded optimized state (classify_ticket.json) might contain stale demos

assertions.name: does_not_rewrite_signature_first
assertions.description: Does not jump to rewriting the signature as the first fix — diagnoses first

assertions.name: provider_agnostic_code
assertions.description: Any dspy.LM() calls include provider-alternative comments

assertions.name: mentions_model_update_possibility
assertions.description: Notes that the model provider may have updated behavior since optimization

evals.prompt: My DSPy pipeline has 3 steps — generate search query, retrieve docs, then answer the question. It runs but the final answer is always wrong or generic. No errors in the console. I think the retriever is broken but not sure. How do I figure out what is going wrong?
evals.expected_output: Step-by-step isolation strategy: test each component individually, starting with inspect_history to see all 3 LM calls. Should show how to call each predictor separately and print intermediate outputs. Should NOT suggest rewriting the whole pipeline.

assertions.name: isolates_components
assertions.description: Suggests testing each pipeline step individually rather than rewriting everything

assertions.name: uses_inspect_history
assertions.description: Uses dspy.inspect_history() to examine what each step produced

assertions.name: checks_retriever_directly
assertions.description: Shows how to test the retriever independently to verify it returns relevant passages

assertions.name: prints_intermediate_outputs
assertions.description: Adds or suggests print statements between pipeline steps to see data flow

evals.prompt:
I just set up DSPy for the first time. I'm trying to run a simple Predict call but I get AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'generate'. My code is:

import dspy
classify = dspy.Predict('text -> label')
result = classify(text='hello world')

What am I doing wrong?
evals.expected_output: Identifies that dspy.configure(lm=...) was never called. Shows the fix with a provider-agnostic LM configuration. Should be concise since this is a simple setup issue.

assertions.name: identifies_missing_configure
assertions.description: Correctly identifies that dspy.configure(lm=lm) is missing

assertions.name: shows_fix_with_configure
assertions.description: Provides working code with dspy.configure() before the Predict call

assertions.name: provider_agnostic_code
assertions.description: Any dspy.LM() calls include provider-alternative comments

assertions.name: concise_response
assertions.description: Does not over-explain or suggest unnecessary changes beyond the missing configure call
